message:
feat(cli): batched blocked-cache refresh with stale-marking fallback and update command error resilience

Blocked cache refresh:
- Add finalize_batched_blocked_cache_refresh() that pre-marks the cache
  stale before attempting a rebuild, so if the rebuild fails (e.g. table
  dropped by concurrent agent), the cache is left in a known-stale state
  rather than silently serving stale data without any marker
- Replace direct rebuild_blocked_cache(true) calls in close, defer,
  undefer, and reopen commands with the new batched finalizer
- Add stale pre-marking to rebuild_blocked_cache_after_partial_mutation
  so partial mutations also leave a breadcrumb on failure
- Add test: finalize_batched_refresh_marks_cache_stale_when_rebuild_fails

Update command error resilience:
- Wrap every mutation step (get_issue, update, add_label, remove_label,
  add_dep, remove_dep, set_parent, clear_parent, add_comment) in
  preserve_blocked_cache_on_error so that if any step fails mid-batch,
  the blocked cache is marked stale before propagating the error
- Track blocked_cache_dirty flag across mutation steps and use it for
  the final batched cache refresh
- Add storage.mark_blocked_cache_stale() method to SqliteStorage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
